description_en: A monument in the natural monument category, the Japanese yew (kyarataboku) of Kabuto Shrine. Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 1992-06-30. Located at Habuta Aza Miyanomae, Nishigō Village.

name_en: Seven-Buddha Yakushi Hall Guarding the Demon Gate
description_en: A tangible cultural property, the Seven-Buddha Yakushi hall serving to guard the demon gate (kimon). Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 1995-02-08. Located at Nagasaka Aza Nagasaka 153, Nishigō Village.

name_romaji: Kyū Gunba Hojūbu Shirakawa Shibu Jimusho Shikichi
name_en: Former Shirakawa Branch Office and Grounds of the Army Remount Depot
description_en: A monument in the historic site category, the former Shirakawa branch office and its grounds of the army remount depot. Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 1997-12-22. Located at Odakura Aza Uenohara 463 and 464, Nishigō Village.

name_en: Nagakura Shrine Listed in the Engishiki
description_en: A monument, Nagakura Shrine, listed in the Engishiki register. Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 1997-12-22. Located at Nagasaka Aza Nagasaka 36, Nishigō Village.
A tangible cultural property, the fire-preventing (hibuse) Kannon hall. Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 1997-12-22. Located at Mafune Aza Kannonyama 3, Nishigō Village.
A monument, the Thirty-Three Kannon. Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 1997-12-22. Located at Mafune Aza Kannonyama 3-2, Nishigō Village.
A monument, the natural habitat of wild skunk cabbage (mizubashō) at Tadogairi. Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 1997-12-22. Located at Mafune Aza Tadogairi 7, Nishigō Village.

description_en: A tangible cultural property, the Mushigasa Dainichi stone monument together with two other monuments. Village-designated by Nishigō Village on 2012-04-27. Located at Habuta Aza Ushikubo 46, Nishigō Village.

A monument in the historic site category, the Gorōshirō milestone mound (ichirizuka). Town-designated by Ōkuma Town on 1973-04-01. Located in Ōkuma Town.

name_romaji: Sōō-ji Yakushi Nyorai Sanzonzō (Yakushi Nyorai Nikkō Gakkō Bosatsu)
name_en: Yakushi Nyorai Triad of Sōō-ji Temple (Yakushi Nyorai, Nikkō and Gakkō Bodhisattvas)
description_en: A tangible cultural property, the Yakushi Nyorai triad of Sōō-ji Temple, comprising Yakushi Nyorai and the Nikkō and Gakkō Bodhisattvas. Village-designated by Ōtama Village. Located at Tamai Aza Minamimachi, Ōtama Village.
